_comment: ADVERSARIAL eval: questions deliberately phrased to share as little vocabulary as possible with the target record's title/aliases/description/service. This tests whether lexical+structured retrieval is genuinely sufficient, or whether semantic embeddings are needed to bridge lexically-disjoint phrasing (the exact case Gate 0's embeddings solved). expectedId references corpus.synthetic.json ids.

questions.q: are we serving responses out of memory or hitting the datastore every time?
why can't people get into their accounts?
are shoppers' purchases getting rejected at the register?
how long until an order is ready to hand off to the carrier?
are the standby copies of the database falling behind the leader?
are outbound alerts stuck waiting to go out?
how much reliability headroom is left this month?
is the streaming pipeline keeping up with everything coming in?
how long do visitors wait at the entrypoint before a service answers?
what's our monthly bill from the cloud provider?
who picks up the phone when something breaks?
are product pages taking too long to render?
